title: Brief DVD question
wikitext:
[]: Brief DVD question             Quote Post by Shiro » Sat Jul 26, 2008 1:33 am I'm hoping to import the DVD for my birthday next week (I even found an in-stock special edition...) but I want to make sure something first. Everyone who imported was able to play it on their computer, right? I talked with Ornette tonight and found out that some DVDs work and some don't. Just making sure it will, without having to get a region-2 DVD player.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jul 26, 2008 1:36 am Even if it doesn't, you can easily rip it, make it region-free, and burn it to another disc.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 26, 2008 2:15 am Mine worked fine on the computer (using DVD Region Free) and, predictably, on the Japanese PS2, as well. NAveryW wrote: Even if it doesn't, you can easily rip it, make it region-free, and burn it to another disc.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Jul 26, 2008 5:35 am Worked fine for me. If it doesn't work you can always change the region settings of your DVD drive by right-clicking it - properties > hardware tab > properties > region - you can change the region code up to something like 5 times. For your DVD player - http://www.dvdhacks.co.uk/

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jul 26, 2008 4:10 pm Question: Does anyone know a way to play another Region DVD on their computer without having to reset the region. In my case my computer plays Region 1 DVD's and I've already made the Region change 4 times so next would be my last. Which is something I definitely DON'T want to do. I have ripped myself a perfect copy of Eva 1.0 from the DVD and have the process down pat but the starting block is to play the DVD off your computer. Now in the case of future R'Eva films I want the same quality and do plan to buy the DVD's. I was just wondering if their was some way (I'm Apple-MacBook Pro) to make it Region Free or some way or form to play another Region DVD one time-so I can rip the files to make a VLC compatible one. Huzzah!

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Jul 29, 2008 9:13 pm This is a bit dangerous to your MacBook , but Mac is like that. It should work, but as the writer says Warning (caps and all) wrote: IF YOU DO THIS, YOU RUN THE RISK OF PERMANENTLY DAMAGING YOUR DVD HARDWARE. TO THE POINT THAT IT WILL NO LONGER WORK. APPLE WILL NOT COVER IT UNDER WARRANTY AND IT WILL COST A LOT TO REPLACE.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 29, 2008 9:36 pm @Papa: Have you looked around for a program? I have a free one called DVD Region Free, and I can play any region DVD while it's open. There's probably something like it for the Mac. There are also some rippers that can remove the region encoding. If this is all redundant crap, suman .

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Jul 29, 2008 9:53 pm Reichu wrote: @Papa: Have you looked around for a program? I have a free one called DVD Region Free, and I can play any region DVD while it's open. There's probably something like it for the Mac.
